title: grumilea microphylla
intro: Grumilea microphylla, also known as the "Small-leaf Grumilea", is a plant species that belongs to the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. Traditionally, Grumilea microphylla has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and acting as an expectorant to help clear congestion. Grumilea microphylla is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
